def: v.t. To swerve from, to disobey, to kick against; to decline, refuse.
more: v. a. 1. To refuse; decline; gainsay. 2. To disobey; set at naught; kick against.

def: s.m. (local) Nonentity, non-existence ( = nāṭh).

v. a. To agitate, to toss, to shake.

def: s.m. Shaking, agitating, agitation, &c.

more: s. m. 1. A hedge-hog. 2. The name of Muhammadʼs mule or of the mule (or horse) of علي aﻌlī, son-in-law of Muhammad. 3. ( met. ) Fat.
subs: 'Rider of duldul,' a title of ʻAlī.

def: s.f. The dolphin (supposed to save one who is drowning).
s.f. The dolphin (supposed to save one who is drowning).
s.m. A bridegroom.

n. m. S. दूर्लभ or उद्वोढा 1. Bridegroom. Ek supārī āle māṅ, dūlhe kī māṅ tāle māṅ . Boys' rhyme. Marriage song. A betel-nut see in the niche, the bridegroom's mother's locked inside. 2. A husband. Tumhāre dūlhā miyāṅ kahāṅ haiṅ? 3. A fop; dandy; swell. Dūlhā banā phirtā hai. dūlhā bhāī , Mah. Brother-in-law ( bahnoī ).
s. m. A bridegroom.
s.m. = dulhā, q.v. s.m. A bridegroom.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀤𑀼𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀳 (dullaha), ultimately from Sanskrit दुर्लभक (durlabhaka, literally "hard to attain or gain"), from दुर्- (dur-, “hardly”) + लभक (labhaka, “attained”), itself a compound of the root लभ् (labh, “to gain, attain”) + -क (-ka, adjectival suffix from Proto-Indo-Iranian *-kas).

def: s.f. = dhulenḍī, q.v. s.f. The first day of the month Ćait (on which it is the practice of the Hindūs to scatter ashes); the second day of the Holī festival (on which they throw a red powder called abīr or gulāl on one another).

n. f. S. धूलि dust, उड्डी to fly. The first day of the month Chait , or the second day of the Holī , on which it is the practice to throw ashes, abīr, gulāl , etc. on one another. The ashes thrown are typical of the dust which begins to be blown at this time.

ety: Prk. दुल्लहिणी and दुल्लहिआ; S. दुर्लभ+इनी and इका
def: s.f. A bride (see dulhā).
n. f. 1. A bride. 2. A wife.

n. f. 1. A bride. Jogī the so ram gaë, pherī de gaë gāoṅ, Buṛhyā kar-ke ṭeran lāge, dulhan miṭ gayo nāoṅ . Doha. The village roamed, the mendicant's now lost to sight, The old dame now she's called who erst was bride behight. 2. A married woman; a feme covert . 1. Gaune kī rāt ko bhor, hī kone meṅ baiṭh rahī dulhī an-bole, Hāth se chhālī chhipāë-ke sundar nār navāe durāë kapole, Dekhan ko juṛ āīṅ, sab tīyī, nanad, jiṭhānī kareṅ yoṅ kalole, Ek haṅse ek bāṅh gahe, ek aṅchrā eṅch-ke ghūṅgaṭ khole . Sundar Singār. Next morn the bride apart and silent in a corner sat, Her bosom with her hands concealed, head bent, cheeks hid she sat, The women 'bout her press, the sisters-in-law with jest her hail, One laughs, one holds her by the arm, one draws aside her veil. 2. A wife ( jorū ). 1. Falāne kī dulhan . 2. Bahut rahī bābal ghar, dulhin! chal tohe pīyā ne bulāī, Kar le singhār, chatar albetī, sājan ke ghar jānā hogā ! Bhajan. Mauj. Your father's house hath seen you long, your husband calls you now, Adorn yourself, bright sprightly one! t'your husband's house now go.

ety: Prk. दुल्लहिणी and दुल्लहिआ; S. दुर्लभ+इनी and इका
def: s.f. A bride (see dulhā).
wety: Feminine of دُولْہا (dūlhā, “bridegroom”).

ety: Prk. दुल्लहिणी and दुल्लहिआ; S. दुर्लभ+इनी and इका
def: s.f. A bride (see dulhā).
s.f. = dulhan, q.v. s.f. A bride (see dulhā).

ety: S. दुलि; Prk.-S. डुलि; prob. fr. S. दुल्
def: s.f. A small turtle; a female turtle.
s.m. = dulīćā, q.v.

ety: prob. corr. fr. g̠ālīća = qālīća, vulg. galīćā
def: s.m. A carpet.

ety: Prk. - S. डुलिका; prob. S. दुल्+इका
def: s.f. A kind of wagtail.

ety: Prk. दुलक्किआ, fr. S. दुल or दोल+कृ; cf. dulakhnā
def: s.f. Trot (of a horse, or dog); — a dog-trot
more: n. f. The trot of a horse or dog. dulkī jā'nā, yā chalnā , v. n. To trot.
s. f. Trot. دلکي جانا يا چلنا dulkī jānā or -chalnā, v. n. To trot.

def: s.m. = dulhā, q.v. s.m. A bridegroom.
s.m. A venomous animal like a spider, a tarantula.
s.m. A purse or bag (worn or carried on the waist), a letter-bag.
v.n. To move, stir, &c. ( = ḍolnā, q.v.); — to walk with a peculiar twisting of the body (a gait much affected by women; — cf. ḍagmagānā).
more: v. n. (dakh.) To move, to be agitated: see ڐولنا ḍolnā.

ety: Pehl. dum; Zend duma, rt. du = S. धू
def: s.f. Tail; end, extremity; — a constant follower (see next)
more: n. f. H. pūṅch , 1. Tail. 2. End; extremity. 1. Hāthī nikal gayā, dum rah gaī . Prov. The elephant's got out, his tail remains. (Said when very little remains to do of a great work). 2. Chhoṭe se Gāzī mīyāṅ, baṛī sī dum! Prov. A warrior short, with such a length of tail! 3. Khaṛā to rah, terī dum meṅ rassī bāṅdhūṅ . Just stand still for a moment and I'll tie a string to your tail. (Said to frighten children away). 4. Pichhle misre kī baṛh gaī hai dum . Inshā. The last verse has too long a tail. 3. A follower; an imitator. dum-dār , adj. Having a tail. dum-dār tārā ; H. pūṅchhal tārā ; Wom. jhāṛū ; A. zū-zanab , n. m. ( Ast .) A comet. dum dabā-kar, yā nok dum bhāgnā , v. n. 1. To run away with one's tail under his legs. 2. To turn tail; show the white feather; slink away; wriggle out of. Phr.—To drop one's tail; hang down one's crest; take to one's heels; save one's bacon; cut and run; f…

s. f. Tail, end, extremity. دم دار dum-dār, Tailed, having a tail. دم دبانا dum dabānā, To run away, to put the tail between the legs. دم دبا جانا dum dabā jānā, To run away. دم علم کرنا dum aﻌlam karnā, To run away. دم لابه dum-lāba, s. The wagging of the tail (as, of a cat or dog when pleased). دم لابه کرنا dum-lāba karnā, To wag the tail (as, a dog or cat when pleased). دم مين گهسنا dum meṅ ghusnā, To take protection.
subs: s.m. Tail of a paper kite; a tail, generally; (met.) one who is ever at the heels of another (as a child that follows its mother about), a constant follower
adv. With the tail between the legs
v.n. To put the tail between the legs, to drop the tail, to turn tail, to run away ( = dum dabā-kar bhāgnā)
adj. In good condition, plump, fat
v.n. 'To make the tail erect'; to run away with the tail stuck out (as an elephant, &c.)
s.m. The root, or place of growth, of the tail (of a bird, or other animal), the coccyx
s.m. The wagging of the tail (as of a dog, when pleased)
To wag the tail ( = dum hilānā)
(-kī), To creep under the tail (of, as pups under a bitch); to take refuge or protection (with); to dangle or run (after), to follow about, to cling (to, as a child to its mother's skirt)
To shake or wag the tail (as a dog, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit दम (dama).

more: (perhaps also dumāﻌ ), s. m. Tears of the eye (from sickness or old age), the sap or water that drips from a vine in spring.

ety: S. डोम+pleon. suff. ār = Prk. आलो = S. आलः
def: s.m. = ḍom, q.v. s.m. A low caste of Hindūs (they make ropes, mats, baskets, fans, &c. &c., and are employed in removing carcases, filth, &c., and about burial and burning grounds: they are also tumblers and merry-andrews; and withal are great thieves: thus it will be seen that, besides the name, they have much in common with the gipsies or 'Rom'); — a caste of Mohammedans (perhaps converts from the Hindū ḍoms), the males of which are musicians, and their women singers, dancers and actresses (but they sing and dance &c. in the presence of women only); — an individual of the ḍom caste.
s.m.f.(?), The glomerous fig-tree, Ficus glomerata.
n. f. H. do two, maṭṭī soil. A mixture of clay and sand; land of the second quality. dūmaṭ-i-arāzī , n. f. G. G. Rich highly cultivated land.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. do. adj. Two

def: A tract of country at the foot of hills, a valley.
adj. Low, base, vile, ignoble, grovelling, mean, paltry, poor, inferior, contemptible
adj. Base; ignoble (H. pājī ); poor. dūṅ-himmat , adj. Mean-spirited; low-minded.
more: n. f. S. दात्र fire. An excess of heat or thirst. dauṅ lagnā , v. n. lit . To be excessively thirsty. 1. To burn with love; pine in absence. Hirde aṅdar dauṅ lagī, dhuāṅ na parghat hoë, Jā tan lāge so lakhe, dūjā lakhe na koë . The flame that's in the heart no smoke at all doth show, He only sees who feels, no other it can know. 2. To long for; crave. Tum ko to iskī dauṅ lag rahī hai .

def: adj. (f. -ī), Two-fold, double, twice as much, twice, multiplied by two ( = dugnā)
more: adj. S. द्विगुण, Pr. dūunā . See دگنا dūnā dū'nā, dugnā dugnā , adv. More and more; all the more; Jūṅ jūṅ manā karo, dūnā dūnā hotā hai . The more you check him the worse he is. dūn kī lenā , v. n. lit . to make oneself out twice as great, etc. To boast; brag. Poṅchhte phirte the kal tak nāk tum, Āj mujh se dūn kī lene lage! Wiping your nose you went about but yesterday, Today your boastful airs before me you display!
n. m. S. दमन, Southern wood; Artemisia lactifolia .

n. m. Leaves folded up in the shape of a cup for holding flowers, sweetmeat, etc. khaṛā daunā denā , v. a. To offer a daunā full of sweetmeats, etc. in fulfilment of a vow; to pay one's vows. Jald bāzār se tū lā daunā, Ki dadā! dāṅgī maiṅ khaṛā daunā . Wom. Raṅgīn. Quick from the market now for me a dauna bring, O nurse! I needs must make my votive offering.
s. m. The name of a flower. (A species of Artemisia).
s. m. Leaves folded up in the shape of a cup for holding betel, flowers, sweetmeats, &c.
subs: dūnā-dūnā, adj. & adv. Twice as much again; more and more, all the more; — s.m. The double of a thing added thereto.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa दूण (dūṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Prakrit 𑀤𑀼𑀉𑀡 (duuṇa), 𑀤𑀺𑀉𑀡 (diuṇa), from Sanskrit द्विगुण (dviguṇa).

def: adj. contrac. of dūnā, q.v.
s.m. A large kettle-drum ( = dundubhī); — noise, din, tumult, uproar, riot; outrage, injustice ( = andher)
more: n. m. S. दुन्दु a sort of drum. 1. A kind of kettle-drum. 2. Noise; yell; scream. Aisā dund mach rahā hai ki koī kisī kī nahīṅ suntā . 3. Riot; tumult. 4. Injustice ( andher ). dund machānā , v. a. 1. To make a noise or riot. 2. To oppress ( andher k. , 1.). Kachahrīyoṅ meṅ ek dund machā huā hai .
s. A large kettledrum. See دندبهي dundubhī.
subs: To make a noise, create an uproar; to perpetrate an outrage, to practise oppression.

def: s.m. = ḍunḍu, ḍunḍubh, q.v. s.m. = dundubhi, q.v. s.m. A large kettle-drum; — an epithet of Krishṇa; — an epithet of an Asura, and of a Rākshas; — dundubhī, s.f. A kettle-drum; — a pair.
s.m. (dialec.), A dust-cloud, a whirlwind.
s.m. A bullock with one horn, or with broken or crumpled horns.
adj. A bullock with one horn, or with bent or broken horns.
more: Panj. n. m. A whirlwind. 1. Āṅdhī chālī, dūṅḍe uṭṭhe, ib mat āg jalā, kāman! Rus. song. 2. Sāsaṛ kā bolā bhūṅḍā rī jīv basole ḍhālāṅ chhole, Gam kā ṭhāve ḍūṅḍā rī . Wom. Rus. song. The mother-in-law's words are awful to bear, As chips from a chisel, or grief's whirlwind to scare!

def: s.m. = dund, dundu, dundubhī.
s.m. A sugar-mill; the outlet for the smoke at a sugar-mill ( = dhundkā).
s.m. = dundubhi, q.v. s.m. A large kettle-drum; — an epithet of Krishṇa; — an epithet of an Asura, and of a Rākshas; — dundubhī, s.f. A kettle-drum; — a pair.
s.m. = ḍunḍubh, q.v. s.m. (f. -ā), A kind of lizard without feet, Amphisbæna; a kind of snake.
s.m. (f. -ā), A kind of lizard without feet, Amphisbæna; a kind of snake.
s.m. A large kettle-drum; — an epithet of Krishṇa; — an epithet of an Asura, and of a Rākshas; — dundubhī, s.f. A kettle-drum; — a pair.
more: n. f. S. दुन्दुभि A mythical drum; celestial music. Deotā maṅgal gā rahe the, dundbī baj rahī thī .

def: s.m. A sort of red worm.
adj. (f. -ī), Deep, profound.
adj. Deep. Bachhṛā bhī tatte nāhīṅ thamtā! Ḍūb kue bhītar ḍūṅghe! Rus. Said by the mother-in-law. The calf even you cant hold in. Would you were drowned in some deep well!
more: n. m. 1. A trough. 2. A canoe; boat. 3. A ladle made of the shell of a cocoanut with a long wooden handle. 4. A hot-water or beefsteak dish; a tureen. 5. An entrèe . ḍoṅgāī, ḍūṅgāī, ḍoṅgān , n. f. Depth. ḍoṅgī, dauṅgī, ḍeṅgī , n. f. A small boat.

ety: ḍoṅgā+ān = Prk. अणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ: S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.m. Depth, deepness; abyss, gulf.
s.f. (local), A small fractional division of an estate.

n. m. Dim. ḍūṅgrī , n. f. 1. A hill; high ground; mount. Daham lage, aur māchhar kāṭeṅ, āṅkh jhapakne pāve nā. Ḍūṅgar ḍhāl kaṭaiṅ saiṅ ratyāṅ, tauṅh auṅṭhī silgāve nā! Rus. To the mother-in-law. It is so hot, mosquitoes bite, sleep hath my eyes forswore, The night sits heavy as a hill, Oh! dont inflame me more! 2. A hilly country. Ḍūṅgar jaṅgal līye binā ke tanne, balam, sare nāhīṅ, Ḍūṅgar par ghās uge nāhīṅ . W. Rus. song. Will nothing serve you, husband, but hill jungle you must take on lease? Nograss upon the hill tops grows.

ety: Prk. डोंगरो; perhaps fr. S. उदग्रः; cf. ḍāṅg
def: s.m. Hill, mountain; hilly country; high ground.
adj. = ḍūṅgā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Deep, profound.
s.f. A coarse kind of cloth.

ety: prob. S. धानक+कः; see dāna, in comp. with which it is com. used
def: s.m. Crumb, scrap, pickings.
more: n. m. Dim. of dānā , q. v. Dānā dunkā .

ety: fem. of ادني adnā, 'nearer,' &c.; rt. دنو 'to be or become near'
def: s.f. (lit. 'Nearer, nearest, former'); the present world, the present life or state of existence; the people of this world, people; a whole world, a multitude; — worldly enjoyments or blessings, worldly goods, the good things of this life, wealth, riches
more: n. f. دنو low. 1. The world and its inhabitants; a multitude; the goods of this world. 1. Dunyā ba-ummed qāyam hai , Prov. The world is sustained by hope. 2. Dunyā khāiye makkar se, roṭī khāīye shakkar se . Prov. The world just cheat, your bread with sugar eat. 3. Dīn se dunyā rakhnī mushkil hai . Prov. To win the world is a harder task than winning Heaven. 2. Wealth; riches. Kaho kitnī dunyā chhoṛī? dunyā-dār , adj. Wordly; secular. duny-dār , n. m. One who is absorbed in worldly affairs. dunyā-dārī , n. f. 1. Secular or worldly affairs. 2. Worldly goods; wife and children. 3. Worldliness. 4. Carnal intercourse. 5. A show of politeness.

(either as being near and present or as worthless, from دنو) s. f. 1. The world. 2. ( met. ) People, goods of the world. دنيا پواتاني پڙنا dunyā po utāne paṛnā (dakh.) To be proud or haughty. دنيا دار dunyā-dār, Worldly, a person of the world. دنيا داري dunyā-dār-ī, s. f. Economy, worldliness, attention to the concerns of the world. دنيا طلبي dunyā-t̤alab-ī, s. f. Seeking the goods of the world, worldliness.
subs: adj. & s.m. Worldly; worldly-minded; — a person of the world; one absorbed in worldly affairs, a mammonist, one possessed of the good things of this world, a rich man
s.f. Worldliness, attention to the concerns of the world; worldly affairs; economy; mammon, worldly goods or possessions, riches; wife and children, family; — a show of politeness
s.f. Seeking the goods of the world, worldliness
The world and whatever is in it.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دُنْیَا (dunyā), borrowed from Arabic دُنْيَا (dunyā), from أَدْنَى (ʔadnā), from دَنِيّ (daniyy). First attested in c. 1435 as Middle Hindi دنیا (dnya).

def: adj. Belonging to the world, worldly, secular.
more: adj. Belonging to this world, worldly.
wety: From دُنْیا (dunyā, “world”) + ـوی (-vī, “-ly”).

def: adj. Belonging to the world, worldly, secular.
more: adj. Belonging to this world; worldly.
adj. Belonging to this world, worldly.

def: s. m. See دوپـﮩريا under دو do.
adj. Pregnant ( = dopastā, q.v.). adj. Pregnant ( = du-jiyā).
s.m. For comp. having du or do for the first member, see s.v. do, 'two.'
more: s. m. See دوپٿه do-paṭṭa under دو.

def: n. m. lit . having two breadths. A cloth thrown loosely over the shoulders; a wrapper. du-paṭṭā tān ke sonā , v. n. 1. To sleep with the sheet drawn over one's head. 2. To sleep with a mind at ease or a light heart. 3. To sleep the sleep of death. du-paṭṭā yā chādar hilānā, yā phirānā , v. a. To hold out a flag of truce; to offer to surrender. du-paṛtā , adj. Of two-folds; double. du pushtah k. , v. a. To print on both sides. du-palṛī , n. f. A native cap consisting of two pieces. du-pal'kā , n. m. lit . having two eyelids. 1. A kind of pigeon. 2. A kind of stone for a ring. do-phār , ( Bot .) Bipartite. do-pahar; Rus. do-pahrī , n. f. ट्ठिप्रहर Midday; noon. Jo yoh līlā du-pahre gāve, Chhaitīs bhojan rasoī meṅ pāve . Bhajan. Who Krishna's praises sings two hours each day, Him six and thirty dishes will repay. do pahar par ānā . ( Ast .) Transit. do pahar pahle , adv. Ante meridiem ; A. M. do pahar chakkar, garmī manḍal , ( Ast .) Meridional lines. do-pahar ḍhal'nā , v. n. To pass the meridian. Ab woh husn nahīṅ rahā, do pahar ḍhal gaī . Your beauty all for aye hath flown, Your noonday waneth to sundown. do pahar ḍhale; Post meridiem ; P. M. do-pahar kī uṅchāī . ( Ast .) Meridian altitude. du-paharyā , adj. Noontide; meridional. du-paharyā , n. m. 1. ( gul-i-dupaharyā ) A flower of a deep carmine color; Pentapetes phœnicea . 2. A child born at noon. 3. A naughty child. du-piyāzā , n. m. A rich meat curry in which a double quantity of onions is used. du-tārā , n. m. A two-stringed instrument. du-taī , n. f. A piece of cloth having two breadths, used for lying upon. do ṭūk javāb denā , v. a. To give a short and decisive answer; settle a matter with a word; to cut the matter short; give a flat denial. do-ṭūk, yā do k. , v. n. 1. To cut in two. 2. To separate; disjoin. do-ṭūk h. , v. n. 1. To be separated, divided, at feud. 2. To be adjusted, settled. do-jīyā ; Sant. bar jīvī , adj. Pregnant; with child. do-jībhā, du-jibhā , adj. Pālī, dvijivho . Double-tongued. do-jī se, yā du-jīyā h. , v. n. To be pregnant. du-chār h , v. n. To meet; have an interview ( āṅkheṅ chār h .). Shab ko diloṅ meṅ wāh! wāh! zor mazoṅ ke tār the! Ham se do chār yār thā, yār se ham do chār the! Nazīr. Last night such streams of pleasure coursed my veins within! Her eyes in mine transfixed, my eyes in hers locked in! du-chāh , The second reservoir to which water is raised for the purpose of irrigation. du-chittā, du-dilā , adj. 1. Of two minds; doubtful; wavering. 2. Abstracted; absent. du-chittā, do-dilā , n. m. Suspense ( dubdhā , 1); absence of mind. du-chand , Poet. du-bālā , adj. Double; two-fold; as much again. du-chand; sazā . ( Law ) Double punishment. du-chobā , adj. A double-poled tent. du-chhattā , A house with a double roof; a thatched roof sloping both ways. du-harfī , (P. maë ), n. f. Wine. du-dastah , adj. Double-handed; having two handles. du-dastah khilāl , ( Cards ). One man winning a slam or rubber from two other competitors. do din kā mehmān , adj. lit . a guest for two days. Transitory; transient; passing away. Husn yeh do din kā hai mehmān, haṅs le, bol le! Nazīr. Beauty's at best a two days' guest, laugh and enjoy the merry jest. do din na pakaṛnā , v. n. To die all too soon. do do dāne ko phirnā , v. n. To beg from door to door for a crust of bread. du-dhārā, du-dhārī , adj. Two-edged (sword). du-rāhā , n. m. A road branching off in two directions; the fork of a road. du-rukhā , adj. See du-bhesyā . 1. The same on both sides; not having a wrong side. 2. Having two colors or aspects. do-ras , n. m. Clay and sandy soil. du-rasā , n. m. A mixture of two kinds of tobacco. du-raggā, doglā , n. m. A mongrel; half-bred. du-raṅgā , adj. 1. Of two colors; piebald. 2. Hypocritical. du-raṅgī, du-ruī , n. f. Duplicity; hypocrisy; double-dealing. Du-raṅgī chhoṛ de, ek raṅg ho jā! Sarāsar mom ho, yā saṅg ho jā! Eschew hypocrisy, one color be! Or be a stone, or soft wax be! du-rūyā , adj. Double-faced. du-rūyā , adv. On both sides. du-rūyā sikhar kāṭ , ( Math .) Hyperbola. du-zānū baiṭhnā , v. n. To sit on one's hams or haunches; to kneel. du-sār honā , v. n. To penetrate; pierce. du-sālā , adj. Biennial. du-sālā , n. m. Lands that have been two years under cultivation. dusrāṅd' , n. f. An inmate of the house; a chum; a fellow-lodger. du-sūtī , n. m. A kind of cloth, the threads of which are double. du-serī , n. f. A weight of two sers. du-shākhā , n. m. 1. The fork of a tree. 2. A pair of legs. 3. Vulva fœminœ. du-shāl'ā; Rus. dusālā , n. m. S. शाढ cloth. A pair of shawls. 1. Kuchh māl dushālā nahīṅ kambal ke agāṛī; Yeh mol meṅ bhārī hai to voh tol meṅ bhārī . Āzād. Devotees. No shawl can with a blanket vie, Less weighty though its price be high. 2. Kamar ṭūṭe ranḍī ki, bhaṛve oṛheṅ dushālā . Prov. The harlot breaks her back, the pimp fine shawls disports. 3. Koi oṛhe sāl dusālā, morā pīavā oṛhe kārī kāmaryā . Wom. Some wear rich shawls, my love a blanket black. dushāle-posh , n. m. One who sports a shawl; hence, a swell. dushāle meṅ lapeṭ-ke lagānā , v. a. To administer sugared poison; to cover one's gall with a coating of honey; cover the point of one's dagger with velvet. du-tarfah, du-tarfī , adj. Having two sides; two-sided; mutual. du-amlī , n. f. The rule of two masters. du-faslī, du-sākhī, du-har, du-phalī, du-tanābī , (opp. of ek-fardah or rice lands). n. f. Land yielding two crops in a year. do karnā , v. a. To divide into two parts; to bisect; cut in two. do kauṛī kī bāt kar denā , v. a. To disgrace oneself; lay one's honor in the dust. du-gānā , n. m. 1. Two prostrations at prayer. 2. Two intimate female friends. 3. A woman who engages with another woman in sexual intercourse with the aid of a dildo. du-ganḍī chiṭṭī , n. A go-between; one who flatters both parties in a dispute. The idiom is taken from a gambling game played with tamarind seeds used as dice. The seed rubbed and marked for this purpose is called chittī , and if it be fraudulently marked in the same way on both sides it is called du-ganḍī. do-ghaṛ , Two vessels full of water placed one over the other. It is a good omen to pass the do-ghaṛ on one's right, and it is always so placed for a wedding party. du-lāvah , n. m. A well with two lāos , or well buckets and ropes. du-lattī , n. f. A kick with the two hind legs of a quadruped. du-lattī mārnā, yā pheṅknā , v. a. To kick out with the two hind legs (esp. a horse). do-laṛā, dolṛā , n. m. 1. A necklace of two strings. 2. A piece of cloth consisting of two breadths. du-laṛā , adj. Of two rows or strings. du-lohī , n. f. A scimitar made of two plates of steel joined together. du-mahlā, du-manzila, du-khanā , adj. Of two floors or stories (a house, etc.); of two decks (a ship). du-mānī, zū-mānī ; H. du-arthī , n. f. A double meaning; a double-enṭendre. du-mūṅhā , E; du-mūī , W. n. lit . having two mouths. A serpent with two mouths. do muṅh haṅs lenā , v. n. To laugh and amuse oneself in moderation, or within conventional limits. Chār meṅ baiṭh-ke haṅs letī hūṅ do muṅh maiṅ bhī , Wom. Rangīn, Jī meṅ khush hotī hūṅ har dam kī mulāqāt se kam . I like to laugh and while away an hour with three or four, The pleasure of the heart is less from meeting, every hour. du-nātī , adj. Double-barrelled (a gun). du-nokī , ( Bot .) Bifid. du-varqī , n. f. lit . a book with two leaves. 1. A small book. 2. ( Fac .) Pudendum muliebre. du-varqī kā sabaq paṛhnā , Slang. v. n. To engage in carnal intercourse. du-hattaṛ , n. m. Beating with both hands. du-hattaṛ mārnā , v. n. To beat with both hands at one time. du-hattī , adj. With both hands. band o bast-i-du-sālā . Biennial settlement.
s.m. corr. of du-paṭṭā, q.v.s.v. do, 'two.' s.m. For comp. having du or do for the first member, see s.v. do, 'two.'
intj. Away! begone! avaunt! — also, contract. of dūr, 'far,' q.v. (used in comp., e.g. dur-bāsī; dur-darśan-yantra)
more: Mah. Wom. intj. Stand off! See alag , intj. dur dur k. , Wom. v. a. To drive or turn out. Dur dur karūṅ, tau dauṛā āve, Khan āṅgan, khan lāhar jāve, Dehal chhoṛ kahiṅ nahīṅ sūtā. 'Kyoṅ sakhī, sājan?' 'Nā sakhī, kūtā .' Riddle Mukarnī. 'Out! out!' I cry, but still to me he 'll running come, Sometimes within, sometimes without he roves playsome, He never leaves the threshold, never sleeps elsewhere. 'A lover, dear?' 'No, dear, the dog who's present e'er.' dur dur phiṭ phiṭ honā , v. n. To be an object of scorn. dur-muë, dur-muī , Mah. Wom. intj. Be off! go away! ' Chiṛyā, chiṛyā, kivāṛ kholyo.' Us ne kahā, 'dur muë! merī āṅkheṅ dukkheṅ .' Children's story.

A prefix. Opp. of su good. dur-āchār , adj. Misconduct ( bad chalnī ). dur-āchārī , n. m. A bad man. dur-bachan , n. m. Bad words; abuse. dur-ba', dublā , adj. Pālī, dubba'o . Lean; weak. dur-jan , n. m. Pālī, dujjano . 1. A bad man. 2. An enemy. dur-gat, dur-dashā , n. m. Pālī, duggatī . See burā hāl , under burā . dur-gam , adj. 1. Difficult of access; inaccessible; unattainable. 2. Deep; profound. dur-gandh , adj. Pālī, duggandhī . Bad smell. dur-labh , adj. Pālī, dullabha . Hard to obtain; difficult to find. dur-matī , adj. Pālī, dummatī . 1. Evil-minded. 2. Foolish.

prep. Bad, evil, ill, hard, unfortunate, with difficulty (the reverse of सु ). درباچيه dur-bāchya ( दुर्वाच्य ) or دربچن dur-bachan ( दुर्वचन ) s. m. Abuse. دربل दुर्बल dur-bal, adj. Weak, nerveless, faint. دربلتا दुर्बलता dur-balatā, s. f. Weakness, faintness. درجن दुर्जन dur-jan, s. m. An enemy. دردن दुर्दिन dur-din, Day of adversity. درنام दुनीम dur-nām, s. m. Infamy.
(contract. of Pers. دور Sans. दूर ) interj. Avaunt!
s. Name of an ear-ring worn on the little ear ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: v.n. To be an object of scorn
v.t. To drive away, to turn out.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian در (dar). Doublet of دوار (dvār).

ety: P. dūr, fr. Zend dūra = S. dūra
def: adj. Distant, remote, far (from, -se), at a distance, a long way off; — s.f. Distance, remoteness ( = dūrī); — adv. Far, afar, far away, to a distance, beyond (with abl.); — intj. = dūr ho, q.v.
more: adj. S. दूर, Pālī, dūro; Z. durā . Far; distant; remote. 1. Aqlmandoṅ kī dūr balā . Prov. Calamity keeps far from the sensible. 2. Apnā ghar dūr se sūjhtā hai . Prov. One sees his own house afar off. (We have all sharp eyes for our own interests). 3. Jinhoṅ ke kān nahīṅ, dūr kī woh sunte haiṅ, Jo kān-vāle haiṅ baiṭhe woh sir ko dhunte haiṅ . The times out of joint. Nazir. Who have no ears hear e'er so far, Who ears possess distracted are. 4. Dūr ke ḍhol suhāvne . Prov. Distant music is sweet. (Distance lends enchantment to the view). 5. Pās kā kuttā, na dūr kā bhāī . Prov. A dog at hand is better than a brother far off. 6. Sab se behtar hai, miyān, sāhab salāmat dūr kī . Prov. Distant acquaintance is best. (Familiarity breeds contempt). 7. Zamīṅ sakht aur āsmān dūr hai . Prov. The earth's too hard, the sky so far! (Ah! whither can I fly!) 8. Āṅkhoṅ se dūr, dil se dūr . Prov. Out of sight…

1. s. f. Distance. 2. adj. At a distance, far, distant, remote. In Sans. the reverse of उप . دورانديش dūr-andesh, adj. Provident, foreseeing, penetrating, circumspect, sagacious, wise, prudent. دورانديشي dūr-andesh-ī, s. f. Foresight, forecast, circumspection, sagacity, wisdom, carefulness. دورباد dūr bād, God forbid. دوربهاگنا dūr bhāgnā, To abstain from, to abhor, abominate, avoid, shun. دوربين dūr-bīn, 1. s. m. A spy-glass, a telescope. 2. adj. Having the quality of shewing a distant object. 3. Far-seeing; ( met. ) prudent, provident, ingenious. دوربيني dūr-bīn-ī, s. f. ety: Zend dusmainyu, i.e. dush+m˚; S. dush+manyu
def: s.m. Enemy, foe, adversary (in polite speech the word is frequently used euphemistically in a peculiar manner, e.g. āp-ke dushmanoṅ-kī tabʻīyat kab-se ʻalīl hai, 'How long have your foes been indisposed?' but implying, 'Since when have you (or has your honour) been indisposed?')
more: n. m. P. dusht bad, man mind, S. दुर्मनाः from दुः wicked, and मनः mind. 1. An enemy ( bairī , 1). 1. Āo, jāo, ghar tumhārā! khānā māṅge dushman hamārā . Prov. My house is yours, come when you please or go, but whoso wants to eat becomes my foe. 2. Udhār dīje, dushman kīje . Prov. Give a loan, an enemy own. 3. Dushmanoṅ ke muṅh meṅ khāk! May my enemies be choked! (May they be powerless!). 4. Dost kā dushman, dushman; dushman kā dushman, dost . Prov. Your friends' foes are your foes, your foe's foes are your friends. 2. Pl. ( In polite language ) Yourself; your honored self; your worship. Āpke dushmanoṅ kī tabiyat kab se alīl hai? jānī dushman , Mortal enemy. ٴaql kā dushman , A stupid man; a fool (احمق).

s. m. f. An enemy, adversary, foe. دشمن جاني dushman-i-jānī, A deadly foe. دشمن کام dushman-kām, A calamity (q. d. the wish of an enemy). دشمن کشي dushman-kush-ī, Killing of an enemy. دشمن گداز dushman-gudāz, Dissolver or destroyer of enemies.
subs: s.m. Dissolver or destroyer of enemies
s.m. 'Foe of knowledge,' a stupid person, a fool.
ex:
کُچھ بات ہَے کِہ ہَسْتی مِٹْتی نَہِیں ہَماری
صَدِیوں رَہَا ہَے دُشْمَن دَورِ زَماں ہَمارا
kuch bāt hai ki hastī miṭtī nahī̃ hamārī
sadiyõ rahā hai duśman daur-i zamā̃ hamārā

There is something about our existence for it doesn't get wiped
Even though, for centuries, the time-cycle has been our enemy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دشمن (dušman). Compare Punjabi دُشْمَݨ (duśmaṇ).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Second, another, other, next, following (day, &c.); — s.m. A second, a match, an equal, a peer; a duplicate
more: adj. S. द्वितीया. 1. Second; next; other. 1. Ye to dūsre tīsre khaṛā hī rahtā hai . He is here every second or third day. 2. Dūsrī bāt dūsre kahte haiṅ . Prov. Any other statement another may make. (I have told you the truth). 2. Duplicate (A. musannā ). 3. Another (اور); separate; distinct. 4. Second; match; equal ( barābar ); peer. dūsrā utār ; A. jazar . ( Arith .) Square root. dusrā tisrā-kar, dubārā tibārā , adv. Repeatedly. dūsrā chaṛhāo ; A. murabba , ( Arith .) The square or 2nd power. dusrā-ke bolnā , v. n. To say again; repeat. dūsrī bāt kahnā , v. a. To contradict oneself; prevaricate; shuffle. dūsrī bār, dubārā , adv. A second time; twice; again; anew; once more. dūsrī hālat yā sūrat pakaṛnā , v. n. To take or assume a different shape; to change; become. dūsre din , Next day; another day; tomorrow; every other day. dūsrī sanad yā kāgaz , n. f. A duplicate of a document. dūsre…
adj. Second, other, an equal, a match. دوسري dūsre, In the second (place), secondly.
subs: s.m. (Arith.), The second power, the square

def: s.m. Messenger, carrier of intelligence, envoy, emissary, ambassador; a go-between, negotiator; an angel who passes between God and man; a secret messenger, a spy.

As weeds the field destroy, so spies good company. dūt-pan , n. m. 1. See jāsūsī . 2. Mischief-making. dūtā'ī , n. f. Embassy.
s. m. An ambassadour, messenger, a go-between, an angel who passes between God and man.

s.f. A female messenger, a confidante, a go-between, a procuress, a bawd; a spy; a gossiping, mischief-making woman, a tale-bearer, mischief-maker.
more: n. f. 1. A female messenger, or spy. 2. A go-between; a procuress ( kuṭnī ). 3. A backbiter. 4. An incendiary; a firebrand. 1. Sās morī dūtī, nanad morī bairan . My mother-in-law's a spy, my sister-in-law my enemy. 2. Dūlī kahe, 'sun re, man-mohan, paṅkh binā maiṅ pakherū uṛāūṅ, Kāgaj kī dūṅ nāo ko sāgar, dārū ke ganj meṅ āg chhipāūṅ, Jal meṅ thal karūṅ, aur pahāṛ meṅ nāo chalāë dikhāūṅ, Jo tiryā paulī nahīṅ jhāṅkat vā ko thelī bajāvāt lāūṅ .' Kabit. The bawd says, 'witching Mohan hear! a wingless bird I fly, Paper I float as an ocean boat, fires hid in powder lie, In water I away can make, on hills a sail you 'll spy, A woman who ne'er looked outside a strumpet's trade shall ply.'
s. f. 1. ( दूती ) A bawd. 2. ( द्युति ) Refulgence.

adj. & adv. Two and two, by twos; being two-fold or double, binary.
more: n. f. 1. A second; another. Jāṛe meṅ rūī yā dūī . Prov. For winter a blanket or a bedfellow. 2. Matching; pairing. 3. Duality. 3. Disunion; enmity.

Mah. n. f. S. दर्वी A large wooden spoon or ladle. 1. Haṅḍyā na ḍoī, muā sārī pat khoī! Prov. Nor pot nor spoon, shamed by this loon! (She is despised for her poverty). 2. Jiske hāth ḍoī uskā sab koī . Prov. Who hath the spoon that serves the food will be by every body wooed. 3. Ḍoīna māro, bachche ko haukā ho jāëgā . Mah. Wom. Dont strike the child with the spoon, he will be hunger-sick. (Contact with the spoon, which is in constant request for stirring the pot, will make the child continually hungry).
1. adj. By twos, being twofold or double, binary. 2. s. f. The being two or twofold; disunion: see بيگانگي begāna-gī.

def: adj. Second; inferior, second sort.
more: n. m. ḍomnī , n. f. In the East, a low caste who make ropes, mats, chicks, baskets, fans, etc., and are also employed in removing dead bodies and carcases. They are generally thieves. In the West the ḍom is a musician, and the female ḍomnī , otherwise called Mīrāsin , is an actress and singer, who performs in private before the women of the family. 1. Sarāhal bahuṛiyā ḍom ghar jāë . Prov. The daughter-in-law so famed for chastity goes to the ḍom's house secretly. 2. Bin bulāī ḍomnī, laṛke bāle sāth āë . Prov. The uninvited songstress see with all her children squatted free. ḍom-panā , n. 1. The arts practised by ḍoms . 2. Adulation ( bhaṭaī , 2, 3).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دُوُم (duwum).

def: pron. (local), This = yěh

The seventh vowel of the Hindi alphabet. It is the termination of the second and third persons singular of the "aorist," as kare from kar do, and, with oṅ, eṅ , and āṅ , of plural nouns, as ghoṛe horses.
more: M. intj. S. अयि, Pr. aī . A vocative particle. e-lo, ello, lo ; Wom. ailo , intj. Lo! hark! listen! look here! hallo! see! Phr. That's a good joke; anything else you would like. Woh bolīṅ, "elo, aur suno, bachche kī bāteṅ!" Wom. She said, "Only listen to this child!"
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit हे (he), loss of 'h' via colloquial standardisation and/or Punjabi influence. Equivalent to Hindi हे (he).

Turn, winding; revolution; involution; convolution; — twist, coil; plait, fold; — entanglement, complication, maze, perplexity, intricacy, ambiguity; hitch, difficulty, trouble; trick (in wrestling, &c.); artifice, stratagem; wiliness, craftiness; duplicity, deceit; — a roll (of cloth, &c.); a screw; a machine
subs: s.m. & adj. Twist upon twist, all convolution; all intricacy, &c.; — involved, intricate, &c.
v.n. To experience trouble, suffer distress or loss
v.n. The twist (of anything) to become loose; a screw to come out
(-meṅ), A difficulty or hitch to arise (in); to occur as a difficulty or obstacle; to become complicated, difficult, or intricate; — to be entangled (in)
v.n. An artifice or stratagem to be successful; to prevail by stratagem, &c.
(-ke), To bind the twists (of a turban) with care
adj. Twisted, convoluted, coiled, spiral; winding, sinuous; intricate, complicated, involved; knotty, difficult; ambiguous; crooked, tortuous; artful, tricky, wily, crafty, deceitful
adj. Coil within coil; involved, entangled, intricate, complex
(-ko), To wind round, to twist; to screw; to circumvent, to deceive
(-meṅ), To entangle (the string of an adversary's paper kite); to throw obstacles or difficulties (in the way of)
(-meṅ; or -se), To practise artifices or tricks, to trick, deceive; to complicate; — to grapple (with, in wrestling)
v.n. To be twisted, &c.; to coil; to writhe; to be perplexed; to fall into difficulties, to sustain a loss
or khul-jānā, v.n. To be untwisted; to be unscrewed
(-se); To entangle (the silk of one's paper kite with that of another kite), to fight paper kites; — to meet, to embrace
peć-tāb, s.m. Twisting and twining; convolution, twisting knots, folds; contortions; restlessness, anxiety, agitation, perplexity, disquietude, distraction, distress; vexation, anger, indignation
v.n. To twist and turn; to writhe; to suffer distress or anxiety; to be vexed.

def: = eṛ, q.v. s.f. Heel ( = eṛī); spur; a riding cane or stick
subs: eṛ lagāna, eṛ mārnā (-ko), To strike (a horse) with the heel; to urge (a horse) on with the heel; to apply the spur, to spur.

def: adv. Here. See بيهان.
adv. Here, &c. = yahāṅ, q.v.

ety: inf. n. iv cf حوط, 'to surround'
def: s.m. Surrounding, enclosing; enclosed space; enclosure; premises; precincts; division of territory, province; boundary, limit; compass
more: n. m. حَوَطَ surrounded. 1. Compass ( gherā ); limit; boundary ( had ). 2. Precincts; premiss; area ( chauk ). * The forty days of severe cold, personified as a wrestler. 3. Enclosure; an enclosed place; fence; compound ( āṅgan ); court-yard; field. 4. Fold; pound; pen ( khaṛak ). 5. Zone; belt; circle; province; territory; tract; domain; presidency ( halqā ). Baṅgāl hātah. ahātah, yā hā'tā kar'nā, yā gher'nā , v. a. 1. To surround; hem in; hedge in; encircle ( ghernā ). Bāgīche kā hātā gher do . 2. To enclose; rail in; wall in. 3. To bind; confine; limit; determine. 4. To define boundaries; appoint limits; bound ( had bāṅdhnā ).

(inf. iv. of حوط) s. f. A surrounding, an enclosure, a fence (but generally applied to a space enclosed or surrounded), comprehension, compass, circuit, besieging. احاطه کرنا iḥāt̤a karnā, To surround, enclose, fence, besiege, blockade, invest, to comprehend.
subs: v.t. To surround, enclose; hem in; invest; confine; fence; to bound, limit; appoint the limits (of, -kā)
(-kā), To surround with a fence, &c., to lay down the limits or boundary (of), &c. See ěḥāt̤a karnā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِحَاطَه (ihāta), borrowed from Arabic إِحَاطَة (ʔiḥāṭa), from أَحَاطَ (ʔaḥāṭa).

ety: inf. n. iv of حق 'To be or become just,' &c.
def: s.m. The act of proving or establishing the truth of a doctrine; manifesting or showing a truth, or right or due; restoring to rights; administration of justice.
more: (inf. iv. of حقّ Heb. He prescribed, &c.) s. m. Act of proving or establishing the truth of a doctrine, restoring to rights, administration of justice.

ety: inf. n. iv of هلك 'to perish'
def: s.m. Destruction, ruin, perdition.
more: (inf. iv. of هلک) s. Destruction, perdition.

ety: inf. n. iv of همل 'to be at large,' &c.
def: Negligence, indolence, carelessness, inattention, delay.
more: s. m. (inf. iv. of همل) Negligence, indolence, remissness, delay, carelessness.

ety: inf. n. iv of همل 'to be at large,' &c.
def: Negligence, indolence, carelessness, inattention, delay.
more: s. f. (inf. iv. of همل) Negligence, indolence, remissness, delay, carelessness.

def: s.m. Setting on fire, burning.
more: (inf. iv. of حرق) s. m. Act of burning or setting on fire.

def: v.n. To make one's vows as a pilgrim, at a certain stage from Mecca appointed for the purpose, preparatory to entrance into the holy city.
more: (inf. iv. of حرم) s. m. Act of forbidding or interdicting, putting on the pilgrimʼs habit at a certain distance from Mecca, determination or resolution. (At a certain distance from Mecca, the pilgrims interdict themselves all worldly enjoyments, &c. There their resolution is fixed to complete the pilgrimage; and, hence, the word means the determination then formed). جامۀ احرام jāma-i-iḥrām, The garment worn by pilgrims on entering Mecca.

s. m. The garment, consisting of two wrappers without seam, worn by pilgrims on approaching and entering Mecca ( Qanoon-e-islam ): see جامهٴاحرام jāma-i-iḥrām, under احرام iḥrām, in the preceding part.

def: s.m.f. Numbering, reckoning; describing; comprehension.
more: (inf. iv. of حصي) s. Numbering, describing, comprehension.

def: s.m. Besieging; circumscribing; restraining.
